basic_details.job_title: IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 200 Trade Apprentice Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR), Department of Atomic Energy
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Shortlisting on ITI marks, Document Verification, Medical Examination
basic_details.short_information: Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR), a Research and Development Centre under the Department of Atomic Energy, Government of India, has invited online applications for engagement of ITI Trade Apprentices at its Kalpakkam campus under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This is Advertisement No. IGCAR/03/2026, offering 200 apprentice seats across 18 trades such as Fitter, Turner, Electrician, Welder, and PASAA. Online registration opened on 20 July 2026 and closes on 19 August 2026. This article covers the trade-wise vacancy breakup, eligibility, age limit, stipend, selection process, and th

application_fee.details: Application fee is not specified in the notification.

age_limit.details: Category Age Relaxation General (as on 19/08/2026) Minimum 18 years, Maximum 24 years SC/ST 5 years relaxation in upper age limit OBC (NCL) 3 years relaxation in upper age limit PwBD 10 years relaxation in upper age limit | Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates is applicable as per Central Government rules.

qualification.details: Candidates must hold an ITI pass certificate in the relevant trade corresponding to the trade applied for, as listed in the vacancy table above. | Applicants who have already undergone, or are currently undergoing, apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 in any Government, PSU, or Private Industrial organization (partially or fully) are not eligible to apply. Candidates awarded grading under the CGPA system must submit a University/Institute-issued certificate converting CGPA into an appropriate percentage. | Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates is applicable as per Central Government rules.

salary_details.details: Stipend rates are as per the Gazette Notification (G.S.R. 610(E) dated 03-09-2025) on the Apprenticeship (Amendment) Rules, 2025, issued by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, and rules applicable from time to time.

selection_process: Applications will be shortlisted on the following basis:

The candidate must possess an ITI certificate in the trade applied for. Candidates are shortlisted for training based on the percentage of marks obtained in their ITI course. In case of a tie in ITI marks percentage
candidates with a higher percentage in 10th standard (8th standard only for Welder
and Plumber) are placed higher in the merit list. If there is still a tie in 10th/8th class marks percentage

the candidate born earlier (older) is given preference. The selected list for each trade is published on the IGCAR website. All shortlisted candidates must appear for Document Verification; failure to do so results in cancellation of candidature. Engagement as an Apprentice is subject to qualifying a standard medical examination prescribed by the Competent Authority.
candidates must execute a Contract of Apprenticeship. On completion of the training period
every apprentice must undergo a Trade Test; only on passing it is a National Apprenticeship Certificate awarded via the Apprenticeship portal.
how_to_apply: Register on the Apprenticeship India portal at https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in for enrollment as an Apprentice. Applications will not be accepted without the unique registration number (e.g. AXXXXXXXXXX) obtained from this portal.
Complete mandatory Aadhaar (e-KYC) verification of your enrollment on the apprenticeshipindia.gov.in portal.
Provide the same registered email ID and mobile number (used during e-KYC registration) in your IGCAR application.

Prepare scanned documents in PDF format before filling the form: (i) Caste/Disability/EWS certificate, and (ii) 8th or 10th class mark sheet as applicable, ITI trade certificate (NCVT/SCVT), semester-wise mark sheet, and consolidated mark sheet scanned as a single PDF.
Submit the application online via www.igcar.gov.in, under the "Opportunities → Recruitment" tab, between 20/07/2026 and 19/08/2026. Only one trade may be applied for.
Check all entered details carefully before final submission — no changes are allowed after submission under any circumstances.
Selected candidates will be provided hostel accommodation on a chargeable basis, subject to availability.
important_instructions: Candidates should submit an online application for one trade only. Applicants who have already undergone or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 elsewhere are not eligible. Selected candidates must submit a Police Verification Certificate from their local police station at the time of Document Verification. Registered email ID and mobile number are mandatory and must remain active until completion of the selection process; no changes are allowed once registered. IGCAR reserves the right to cancel or alter the number of seats in a trade, or the entire engagement process, without assigning any reason. Disrespectful, aggressive, dishonest, or evasive behavior by an apprentice can lead to termination of the apprenticeship. There is no provision for employment after completion of the apprenticeship training, and candidates cannot claim employment on this basis. IGCAR is governed by special provisions under the Atomic Energy Act, 1962; apprentices are governed by IGCAR's prevailing rules and the CCS (Conduct) Rules, 1964, in matters of conduct and discipline. If any declaration by a candidate is found false, they will be disqualified/terminated at any stage without notice. Canvassing or personal follow-up to influence selection leads to immediate cancellation of candidature. All correspondence relating to the recruitment process will be published only on the IGCAR website — no individual correspondence will be entertained. In case of any dispute, the decision of IGCAR is final.

rows.details: Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Research (IGCAR), Department of Atomic Energy
IGCAR, Kalpakkam, Chengalpattu District, Tamil Nadu – 603102
Shortlisting on ITI marks, Document Verification, Medical Examination

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Trade Apprentice Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: IGCAR is offering 200 apprentice seats across 18 trades. The category-wise distribution is as follows: Of the 200 seats, 8 vacancies are identified for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) as per extant Government of India orders: 2 in Fitter (Dwarfism/Acid Attack Victim/Leprosy Cured), 1 in Turner (Specific Learning Disability), 1 in Machinist (Low Vision), 1 in Electrician (Hard of Hearing), 1 in Instrument Mechanic (Specific Learning Disability), 1 in Plumber (Low Vision), and 1 in Welder (Hard of Hearing). For PwBD candidates, disability must be 40% or more.

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must hold an ITI pass certificate in the relevant trade corresponding to the trade applied for, as listed in the vacancy table above. Applicants who have already undergone, or are currently undergoing, apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 in any Government, PSU, or Private Industrial organization (partially or fully) are not eligible to apply. Candidates awarded grading under the CGPA system must submit a University/Institute-issued certificate converting CGPA into an appropriate percentage. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates is applicable as per Central Government rules.

rows.age_relaxation: 5 years relaxation in upper age limit
3 years relaxation in upper age limit
10 years relaxation in upper age limit

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Apprentice Stipend 2026
content_sections.text: Stipend rates are as per the Gazette Notification (G.S.R. 610(E) dated 03-09-2025) on the Apprenticeship (Amendment) Rules, 2025, issued by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, and rules applicable from time to time.

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Application fee is not specified in the notification.

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Applications will be shortlisted on the following basis: The candidate must possess an ITI certificate in the trade applied for. Candidates are shortlisted for training based on the percentage of marks obtained in their ITI course. In case of a tie in ITI marks percentage, candidates with a higher percentage in 10th standard (8th standard only for Welder, Carpenter/Wood Work Technician, Mason/Civil Mistry, and Plumber) are placed higher in the merit list. If there is still a tie in 10th/8th class marks percentage, the candidate born earlier (older) is given preference. The selected list for each trade is published on the IGCAR website. All shortlisted candidates must appear for Document Verification; failure to do so results in cancellation of candidature. Engagement as an Apprentice is subject to qualifying a standard medical examination prescribed by the Competent Authority. Before commencement of training, candidates must execute a Contract of Apprenticeship. On completion of the training period, every apprentice must undergo a Trade Test; only on passing it is a National Apprenticeship Certificate awarded via the Apprenticeship portal.

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Candidates must bring the following (original plus one set of self-attested photocopies) at the time of Document Verification: Signed printout of the online application form with a recent passport-size photograph pasted on it Police Verification Certificate from the local police station at the place of residence Date of Birth Certificate/SSC certificate 8th/10th standard mark sheets prior to the ITI course ITI trade certificates (NCVT/SCVT) with year-wise mark sheets and consolidated mark sheet Caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL) in the prescribed format, valid as on 19-08-2026, if applicable Valid Income and Assets Certificate as on 19-08-2026, for EWS category Disability Certificate for PwBD (above 40%), if applicable Savings Bank Account number and passbook Aadhaar Card of the applicant PAN Card of the applicant Confirmation copy of Apprentice Registration through the apprenticeshipindia.gov.in portal CGPA-to-percentage conversion certificate from the University/Institute, for applicants graded under the CGPA system, if applicable No TA/DA is paid for attending document verification or the medical examination.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Register on the Apprenticeship India portal at https://www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in for enrollment as an Apprentice. Applications will not be accepted without the unique registration number (e.g. AXXXXXXXXXX) obtained from this portal. Complete mandatory Aadhaar (e-KYC) verification of your enrollment on the apprenticeshipindia.gov.in portal. Provide the same registered email ID and mobile number (used during e-KYC registration) in your IGCAR application. Prepare scanned documents in PDF format before filling the form: (i) Caste/Disability/EWS certificate, and (ii) 8th or 10th class mark sheet as applicable, ITI trade certificate (NCVT/SCVT), semester-wise mark sheet, and consolidated mark sheet scanned as a single PDF. Submit the application online via www.igcar.gov.in, under the "Opportunities → Recruitment" tab, between 20/07/2026 and 19/08/2026. Only one trade may be applied for. Check all entered details carefully before final submission — no changes are allowed after submission under any circumstances. Selected candidates will be provided hostel accommodation on a chargeable basis, subject to availability.

content_sections.heading: IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates should submit an online application for one trade only. Applicants who have already undergone or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training under the Apprentices Act, 1961 elsewhere are not eligible. Selected candidates must submit a Police Verification Certificate from their local police station at the time of Document Verification. Registered email ID and mobile number are mandatory and must remain active until completion of the selection process; no changes are allowed once registered. IGCAR reserves the right to cancel or alter the number of seats in a trade, or the entire engagement process, without assigning any reason. Disrespectful, aggressive, dishonest, or evasive behavior by an apprentice can lead to termination of the apprenticeship. There is no provision for employment after completion of the apprenticeship training, and candidates cannot claim employment on this basis. IGCAR is governed by special provisions under the Atomic Energy Act, 1962; apprentices are governed by IGCAR's prevailing rules and the CCS (Conduct) Rules, 1964, in matters of conduct and discipline. If any declaration by a candidate is found false, they will be disqualified/terminated at any stage without notice. Canvassing or personal follow-up to influence selection leads to immediate cancellation of candidature. All correspondence relating to the recruitment process will be published only on the IGCAR website — no individual correspondence will be entertained. In case of any dispute, the decision of IGCAR is final.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 200 Trade Apprentice vacancies across 18 trades under Advertisement No. IGCAR/03/2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 19 August 2026. Registration opened on 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the stipend for IGCAR trade apprentices?
faq.answer: Apprentices who completed a 2-year ITI training course receive ₹11,040 per month, and those who completed a 1-year ITI training course receive ₹10,560 per month.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 24 years as on the closing date of application (19/08/2026), with relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and 10 years for PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is not specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the qualification required for IGCAR Trade Apprentice posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold an ITI pass certificate in the trade they are applying for.

faq.question: Q7. How can I apply for IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must first register on the Apprenticeship India portal (apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) and complete Aadhaar e-KYC verification, then submit the application online on www.igcar.gov.in between 20 July and 19 August 2026. No offline applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for IGCAR Apprentice Recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is based on shortlisting by ITI marks percentage (with 10th/8th class marks and age as tie-breakers), followed by mandatory Document Verification and a Medical Examination.

faq.question: Q9. Where will the IGCAR apprentice training take place?
faq.answer: Training will be conducted at IGCAR, Kalpakkam, Chengalpattu District, Tamil Nadu – 603102, for a duration of one year.

faq.question: Q10. Is there a job guarantee after completing the IGCAR apprenticeship?
faq.answer: No. The notification explicitly states there is no provision for employment on completion of the apprenticeship, and candidates cannot claim employment on this basis.
